insight:
Yesterday was a solid rest day with your body showing excellent recovery signs! With 6,987 steps and no formal training, you burned 2,399 calories while keeping things light just 4 days out from Chester. Your resting heart rate of 44 bpm shows you're in fantastic aerobic shape, and that low stress level of 30/100 indicates you're mentally relaxed heading into race week.

Your HRV of 118ms is sitting nicely above your baseline range of 93-116ms, which is exactly what we want to see pre-race. This elevated reading, combined with your low resting HR, suggests your nervous system is well-recovered and primed. Stick to your planned easy sessions today - no need to modify anything based on these stellar recovery markers.

With only 6.7 hours of sleep though, prioritize getting 8+ hours tonight. Your body is clearly recovering well, but extra sleep will help lock in those gains and keep your immune system bulletproof for race day.
